name: DuoSkin
short_description: A fabrication process for making jewelry-like on-skin interfaces from gold leaf that sense touch, display thermochromic output, and communicate over NFC.
long_description: DuoSkin is a research prototype and fabrication method that lets people create customized functional devices applied directly to the skin like temporary metallic tattoos, using inexpensive, skin-friendly gold leaf as the conductive material. Gold-leaf traces support three interaction modalities: capacitive touch input (buttons, sliders, 2D trackpads), thermochromic color-changing displays driven by resistive heating, and wireless NFC communication via custom gold-leaf coils. As a piece of physical AI / interaction-design work it matters because it reframes the body itself as an aesthetic, intimate interface surface, pushing wearable computing toward the most proximal possible embodiment while foregrounding ornamentation and self-expression rather than utilitarian gadgetry.
ai_note: DuoSkin is a materials-and-fabrication technique for on-skin sensing, thermochromic actuation, and NFC; no AI or machine learning runs in the system.

images.caption: Gold-leaf DuoSkin on-skin devices worn as jewelry-like temporary tattoos, supporting touch input, thermochromic display, and NFC.
images.alt_text: Close-up of a person's skin decorated with metallic gold temporary-tattoo patterns that function as on-skin electronic interfaces resembling jewelry.

sources.title: DuoSkin: Rapidly Prototyping On-Skin User Interfaces Using Skin-Friendly Materials
